skill.name: testing-anti-patterns
skill.description: Guides test authors away from fragile mock-focused tests, test-only production methods, incomplete mocks, and late testing habits.
skill.summary: Improve test quality by checking real behavior and using mocks only when they preserve the test intent.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown prose and TypeScript examples about testing anti-patterns. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown fences or TypeScript testing examples. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands and contain no command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation blocks, test snippets, or fence delimiters in SKILL.md. There is no runnable script file, shell command, or user-input execution sink.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Testing Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are words such as methods, destroy, or cleanup inside testing guidance. They are unrelated to hashes, encryption, or cryptographic APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context discusses test-only methods and cleanup helpers. No MD5, SHA-1, cipher, key handling, or cryptographic implementation appears at the cited locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Mocking Guidance Flagged as Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to a warning about mock setup complexity. It contains no host discovery, port scanning, network request, or external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 258 is plain testing guidance about excessive mocks. It has no network primitive, URL, IP address, scanner command, or data exfiltration behavior.

content.user_title: Improve Tests by Avoiding Mock Anti-Patterns
content.value_statement: Fragile tests often verify mocks instead of real behavior. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward TDD, complete mocks, and production-safe cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Identifies tests that assert mock presence instead of user-visible behavior.
Guides cleanup logic into test utilities instead of production classes.
Explains how to mock only after understanding required side effects.
Checks whether mock responses match the complete real data structure.
Encourages TDD before implementation is called complete.
limitations: It provides testing guidance, not an automated test runner or linter.
It does not inspect runtime coverage or execute project test suites by itself.
It assumes the user can provide relevant test files and dependency context.
It focuses on mock and TDD issues, not every possible testing concern.

use_cases.title: Review a New Unit Test
use_cases.description: Check whether a test verifies real behavior, avoids mock-only assertions, and uses mocks at the right dependency level.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Production APIs
use_cases.description: Move test-only cleanup methods out of production classes and into dedicated test utilities.

use_cases.title: Improve Team Testing Standards
use_cases.description: Create clear review criteria for mocks, integration tests, and TDD completion expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single test file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this test for testing anti-patterns. Tell me if it tests real behavior, mock behavior, or production-only cleanup concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Find Mocking Risks
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before adding or changing mocks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect these tests and explain which mocks are necessary, which are incomplete, and which may hide required side effects.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Test Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup of test-only production methods.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find production methods that exist only for tests. Propose a test utility approach that preserves behavior without polluting production APIs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a TDD Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TDD plan for this change. Include the first failing tests, minimal implementation steps, mock boundaries, and integration coverage.

output_examples.input: A component test asserts that a sidebar mock test ID is visible.
output_examples.output: The test is checking mock presence, not page behavior. Replace the assertion with user-visible navigation behavior or unmock the sidebar.

output_examples.input: A session class has a cleanup method used only in after-each test hooks.
output_examples.output: This is production API pollution. Move cleanup into a test utility that understands the workspace lifecycle.

output_examples.input: A mocked service response only includes the fields used by the current assertion.
output_examples.output: The mock is incomplete. Compare it with the real response shape and include fields downstream code may consume.

best_practices: Write the failing test before adding mocks or implementation code.
Mock the slow or external boundary, not the behavior the test depends on.
Keep production classes free of methods that only support tests.
anti_patterns: Asserting that a mock component or mock test ID exists.
Adding cleanup or destroy methods to production classes only for tests.
Creating partial mock responses without checking the real data structure.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives review guidance and prompts. The user or agent must run the project test command separately.

faq.question: Can I still use mocks?
faq.answer: Yes. Use mocks when they isolate a real boundary and preserve behavior required by the test.

faq.question: What is the main warning sign?
faq.answer: A test is suspicious when it passes because a mock exists, not because real behavior works.

faq.question: How should test-only cleanup be handled?
faq.answer: Place cleanup in test utilities or fixtures instead of adding unused methods to production classes.

faq.question: Is this skill only for TypeScript?
faq.answer: No. The examples use TypeScript, but the testing principles apply across languages and frameworks.

faq.question: When should integration tests replace mocks?
faq.answer: Use integration tests when mock setup becomes complex or when behavior depends on multiple real components.
